title: More People Steal Gas Because of War
summary: A new report shows that more people are stealing gas from stations. The number of thefts has gone up by 62% compared to last year. This is happening because the war between the US, Israel, and Iran has made fuel prices higher. A gas station owner named Josh says he loses about £2,000 each week from people driving away without paying. He has five stations in England and sees about five thefts per week at each one. Videos show different people stealing gas at all times of day, like on a motorbike or in a van. The police say they are working to stop these crimes. Most thefts are planned, not because people are desperate. The average cost of each theft has also increased, making it a big problem for station owners.

keywords.explanation: when people take something without paying
the cost of gas or diesel
when people fill their car with gas and leave without paying

questions.question: How much has gas theft increased compared to last year?
Why are more people stealing gas?
How much money does Josh lose each week from thefts?
How many thefts does Josh see per week at each of his stations?
What do the police say about the thefts?
options: They are working to stop them
questions.correct_answer: They are working to stop them

questions.question: Are most thefts because people are desperate?

background_read: Gas stations sell fuel for cars. Sometimes, people take fuel without paying, which is called theft. Wars can make things like fuel more expensive because it's harder to get. When prices go up, some people might steal to save money. Police try to catch thieves to keep everyone safe. Station owners like Josh use cameras to watch for thefts and report them.
Article_Structure: WHO: Gas station owners like Josh and police
WHAT: More people are stealing gas without paying
WHERE: At gas stations in England
WHY: Because the war has made fuel prices higher

perspectives.description: Josh is worried about losing money and says most thefts are planned, not desperate.
